title: VOIP/PBX integration
excerpt: Does some one has a good suggestion how to integrate Monday CRM with a proper PBX/VOIP application in such a way contacts can be called (By PC) straight from the CRM and calls are logged and connected to the contact pers…

posts.author: PJSamcMD2021 (Paul Swaak)
posts.cookedHtml:
Does some one has a good suggestion how to integrate Monday CRM with a proper PBX/VOIP application in such a way contacts can be called (By PC) straight from the CRM and calls are logged and connected to the contact person?

If so pls let me know how you did this integration and with which VOIP vendor.

Rgds Paul Swaak

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @PJSamcMD2021 - Welcome to the community - you have great questions.

You can set up VoIP as your default calling software on your PC in most cases, and when you click on the phone number field in monday․com, it can launch your VoIP. We use RingCentral but I believe many of them will work the same way.

As far as bringing the call record back into monday․com, you would need to do that with an integration through something like Integromat or Zapier. We’ve built similar systems for our clients for their SMS text messaging on platforms like Twilio. As long as your VoIP platform of choice allows integration and can provide the data back to other systems, you should be able to bring the logged call into monday․com just fine.

~ Deb Cinkus, PMP
Certified monday․com Partner
CEO & Principal Consultant, Polished Geek

Book a Consultation with a monday․com expert: https://meet.polishedgeek.com/more-with-monday

posts.author: vigneshb (Vignesh B)
posts.cookedHtml: You can integrate Monday CRM with a VoIP solution like TeleCMI to enable Click-to-Call and automatic call logging. TeleCMI offers API-based integration, ensuring that all calls are logged directly within Monday CRM and linked to the right contacts. Let me know if you need guidance on setting this up!

posts.author: daneil (daneil)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Paul,

Excellent question!

Using VoIP/PBX with Monday can certainly automate your workflow. Here are a few things you may want to consider:

Look in the Marketplace: Begin by searching for any pre-existing VoIP integrations within the Monday Marketplace. Sometimes, larger providers have pre-built solutions.

Use Zapier or Make: If direct integration is not available, you can utilize Zapier or Make (previously Integromat). You can create automations to save the calls in Monday as soon as they occur in your VoIP system.

Custom API Integration: If you require a custom solution, you can use both the API of Monday and your VoIP provider. This involves coding but gives you the greatest flexibility.

Some VoIP options to utilize:

RingCentral: Famous for CRM integrations.

MirrorFly: VoIP Api Support

Aircall: Support and sales teams focused.

Do not forget to verify if the vendor supports webhooks for live updates and offers quality documentation!

Hope this was helpful! In case you need more questions, feel free to ask.
